hypotheses.skeptical_risk: Rare events and particle grouping can inflate apparent precursor skill.
hypotheses.statement: Prior-cycle optical features contain weak but nonzero warning signal for abrupt degradation events.

hypotheses.skeptical_risk: Protocol or frame-count artifacts can create synchronized apparent events.
hypotheses.statement: The same-cycle abrupt optical drops around cycles 86 and 116 are coordinated degradation events rather than independent particle noise.

hypotheses.skeptical_risk: Only four example particles are currently summarized, limiting spatial inference.
hypotheses.statement: Particles and regions differ in degradation mode, with synchronized events superimposed on heterogeneous gradual drift.

hypotheses.skeptical_risk: Observable compression may miss spatial crack morphology or phase-front movement.
hypotheses.statement: For this project stage, forecasting physical observables and event hazards is more scientifically useful than unconstrained pixel rollout.

hypotheses.skeptical_risk: Apparent coefficients can be physically misleading without calibration.
hypotheses.statement: Phase-boundary/front-motion and apparent diffusion extraction should be treated as readiness-ranked proxies until visual validation and spatial calibration are complete.

hypotheses.skeptical_risk: Frame-count association may be an imaging artifact, not electrochemical degradation.
hypotheses.statement: Synchronized event cycles are associated with unusually short cycle-frame regimes and protocol context changes.
